title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live voice models that allow simultaneous listening and speaking
summary: OpenAI's new voice models for ChatGPT allow more natural real-time conversations.

title: AMD Zen 6 Medusa Point with 10 cores outperforms Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 in performance tests
summary: The new AMD Medusa Point processor records up to 22% more performance than its predecessor in Geekbench.

title: Nvidia presents GeForce Trading Cards Series 1 collectibles
summary: Nvidia will distribute free collectible cards at events featuring games, GPUs, and tech demonstrations.

title: AMD revives Zen 2 Ryzen 7 4700LE processor in a pre-assembled PC with RTX 3050
summary: The old AMD chip resurfaces in an $800 budget PC with DDR4 memory.

title: Professor suspected AI cheating and grades plummeted after in-person exam
summary: Only two students maintained their grades after switching from an at-home exam to an in-person one.

title: Intel prepares Nova Lake-S CPUs with up to 28 cores for Dunlow workstations
summary: The platform will feature an LGA1954 socket and is expected for the high-performance segment.

title: China accuses Claude Code of containing backdoors; Anthropic denies
summary: The Chinese government claims the code assistant sends information without consent, while Anthropic rejects the accusations.

title: New 'HalluSquatting' attack exploits AI hallucinations to execute malicious code
summary: Attackers take advantage of the tendency of AI assistants to hallucinate to install malware.

title: Meta will use public Instagram photos by default to train its AI tool Muse
summary: The company automatically enables the use of user images in its AI image generator.

title: Google launches 'Video Remix' AI tool in Google Photos
summary: The new feature allows remixing videos using artificial intelligence directly from the app.

title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, an 'Opus-class' AI model at a competitive price
summary: Elon Musk presents Grok 4.5 as a high-performance model at a lower cost than GPT-5.5 and Fable 5.

title: Memory shortage drives up RAM prices and collapses the budget smartphone market
summary: DDR4 prices rise more than 50% in Q3 and memory accounts for up to 64% of the cost of cheap phones.

title: TCL launches gaming monitors with extreme refresh rates: 1080Hz and 320Hz in Mini LED panels
summary: The TCL 27P2A Ultra offers 1080p at 1080 Hz mode, while another model combines 4K and high refresh rate.

title: Open source AI tool Ollama raises $65 million
summary: Ollama reaches nearly 9 million users after a Series A funding round.

title: European Parliament approves Chat Control 1.0 amid privacy criticisms
summary: The law allows scanning communications to detect illegal content, raising concern among digital rights advocates.

title: John Deere owners gain right to repair their equipment under FTC agreement
summary: The agreement allows farmers to repair their tractors without relying exclusively on the manufacturer.

title: Google's deepfake detector debunks a fake image of Mitch McConnell
summary: Google's artificial intelligence tool identified a photomontage of the US politician.

title: United States deploys a projectile-free anti-satellite weapon
summary: The new weapon uses non-kinetic technologies to incapacitate satellites in orbit.

title: ASML solidifies as the world's most important monopoly in chip manufacturing
summary: The Dutch company dominates the production of lithography machines necessary for the most advanced semiconductors.

title: Mistral AI launches Robostral Navigate, an 8B parameter model for robotic navigation
summary: The model allows robots to navigate using a single camera, marking Mistral's entry into robotics.
